basics.name: Lorenzo Dentis
basics.label: Cybersecurity OT Consultant
basics.summary: OT Cybersecurity Consultant with experience in complex industrial environments, specializing in IDS, Secure Remote Access, and ISA/IEC 62443 and NIS2 compliance. Academic background in Security, Networking, and Systems with a solid foundation in distributed architectures and Linux systems. Practical approach focused on a deep understanding of systems: from virtualization to industrial protocols, I enjoy taking things apart to truly understand how they work so I can improve, recreate, and secure them

work.name: Spike Reply
work.summary: Supporting industrial clients in building and maintaining secure OT networks.
highlights: Installation and maintenance of Secure Remote Access solutions (Wallix)

Deployment and maintenance of Intrusion Detection Systems (Claroty and Nozomi): configuration of physical and virtual probes, network device setup for traffic collection, ongoing maintenance and troubleshooting
SOC activities: intrusion monitoring and detection using the aforementioned tools
Compliance consulting on ISA/IEC 62443, NIS2, and MITRE ATT&CK frameworks
Security assessments in industrial plants: network infrastructure analysis, physical security review, compliance interviews, and penetration testing on relevant devices

work.name: Arcese Trasporti
work.summary: Curricular internship as part of the Master's thesis project.
highlights: Migration of a batch-based system (Java Spring) to a stream-based architecture (Apache Flink), as part of a broader transition from a monolithic system to an event-driven microservices architecture

work.name: SUSE
work.summary: Curricular internship as part of the Bachelor's thesis project.
highlights: Research on tracing overhead introduced in virtualized environments (Xen and KVM)

education.area: Computer Science – Security, Networks and Systems
courses: Thesis: Velocity Project – Migration from a legacy system to an Event-Driven microservices architecture (Java Spring, Apache Flink)
Thesis: Comparing KVM and XEN tracing overhead – Supervisor: Prof. Enrico Bini

projects.name: Homelab
projects.description: Ongoing management of a multi-server homelab built on repurposed hardware, used as a hands-on lab to experiment with real technologies. Focus areas include networking (VLAN, DNS, DHCP, VPN with OpenVPN), infrastructure monitoring with Grafana, and service orchestration via Docker Compose/Swarm. The lab also hosts MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers for integrating external tools with LLMs.
